pages.raw_text:
The Washington Water Power com
pany attempted this morning TO spring
a coup d'etat on the city and sent a
force of 40 men to Third avenue where
this company and the Traction com
parry are contending for franchise.
but before they had been able to stick
a pick into the ground the police had
discovered the trick and had a force
Of men on hand to stop it. The result
was that the company's men were call
ed in and the street remains as it was
heretofore.


The board was notified this morning
that the company had men On the
ground with shovels and picks. ready to
go TO work. Officers MeDermott, Dunn
Bucholz and Jellactly under orders
from Captain CoVerly were hurried to
the scene to see that no ground on
the street was broken. SO quick were
the police that they arrived almost as
soon as the men.


Corporation Counsel Judscn sent a
communication to the board this morn
ing saying the Washingtn Water Power
company would be allowed to haul
material to the ground pending the
hearing of the restraining order by
Judge KEENAN,. Saturday he had for
hidden this


mo Thought of It."


H. NJ. Stephens, attorney for the
streetcar company. said this morning:
Eve have no intention of laying track
or digging up the street until we obtain
permission from the court. The hear
ino for the restraining order against
the city will come before Judge Ken
nan next Monday. and as l am of the
opinion that the franchise granted to
the company in 1890 is valid l think
the restraining order will he decided in
our favor. If it is we will start work
Immediately. Personally, do not think
a line is needed on Third avenue. but
if the people demand one we are will
